title: Calocephalus brownii
taxonomy: Calocephalus brownii is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread plant families in the world, comprising over 23,000 species. Calocephalus brownii is further classified within the genus Calocephalus, which is a group of shrubby plants native to Australia. The genus Calocephalus is classified within the subfamily Gnaphaloideae, a subfamily of plants characterized by their small, daisy-like flowers. Calocephalus brownii is a specific species within this genus and family, known for its unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Calocephalus brownii, also known as Brown's silver bush, is a shrubby plant native to Australia, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and sore throats, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calocephalus brownii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calocephalus brownii is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used in the form of a tincture or extract to alleviate respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
